behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (git-derived branch facts + PR-state check + age threshold), not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: no explicit requirement for real network/OS in this behavior text; a local in-process fixture repo model and stub PR provider can expose logic bugs. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text, (b) no AI output, (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejection-gate: System was considered, but behavior text does not require live repo/HTTP; it specifies correctness of classification criteria.
behaviors.test_description: Wire branch-state computation inputs (ahead/behind, last-commit age, PR-open flag) through the real staleness service and assert stale=true only when all three conditions hold; include boundary age values and mixed PR states.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Adversarial critique: if staleness logic is entirely one pure predicate with already-computed inputs, Unit could be sufficient; this label assumes real risk is incorrect module wiring of those inputs.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is bootstrap orchestration (session init + staleness service + warning renderer), so single-function Unit tests can miss wiring failures. REAL-INFRA: behavior text does not require real shell/session process; in-process bootstrap invocation can catch missing emission/context bugs. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real network/OS requirement stated, no AI dependency, no multi-step agent pipeline. Rejection-gate: System was considered, but no concrete text requires validating real subprocess/session manager behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke session-start setup with a stale branch state fixture and assert one warning is emitted containing branch name, age, and next action (e.g., open PR). Also verify no warning for non-stale state in same wiring path.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Adversarial critique: if bootstrap only calls a single formatter function, Unit might catch it; this level assumes defects are likely in cross-module startup wiring.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is explicitly one local deterministic mapping function. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: higher levels are disqualified by behavior text ('local ... function' and 'deterministically').
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven test of the classifier with explicit input states and exact expected labels, including edge combinations and unknown/default handling.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Adversarial critique: if classifier depends on external config loading at runtime, Integration could be needed; behavior text frames it as local deterministic function, so Unit remains minimum.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly says 'against live repo and PR state', requiring real git/repo state plus real PR-system interaction; mocks can hide integration failures in auth, API semantics, branch mapping, and render path. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) yes real infra required -> System. (b) no AI output dependency. (c) no multi-agent chain required.
behaviors.test_description: Run `/kaizen-wip` in a real test repository context with actual PR backend state (or real staging equivalent): case A branch clean, case B branch ahead of main with no PR and stale age. Assert rendered output labels and stale marker are correct in both cases.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Adversarial critique: if 'live' were relaxed to fixtures, Integration could suffice; the explicit 'against live repo and PR state' keeps System as minimum.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: risk is in combining PR status interpretation with stale classification logic across modules. REAL-INFRA: behavior does not require live PR API; fixture PR states (open, merged, none) are enough to expose false-positive logic bugs. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no explicit real OS/network requirement, no AI dependency, no multi-agent sequencing. Rejection-gate: System considered, but behavior text does not require live repo/HTTP, only correct classification by PR state.
behaviors.test_description: Use real classifier/service wiring with PR-state provider test doubles returning open/merged/none for identical branch age/commit conditions; assert only 'none' can become stale-no-pr.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Adversarial critique: if merged/open semantics depend on real API edge fields, System might be needed; absent that explicit requirement, Integration is minimum.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: rebase/squash semantics are git-history operations best validated on a real repository with actual commit graph rewrites; in-process fakes often miss ancestry/merge-base edge behavior. MOCK-HIDE: mocked commit IDs can pass while real rewritten DAG logic fails. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) yes real subprocess/filesystem git behavior is central -> System. (b) no AI dependency. (c) no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Create real git history scenarios: branch commits, PR merged via squash/rebase-equivalent history rewrite, then recompute staleness on rewritten branch/main graph. Assert branch is not classified stale-no-pr after merge outcome despite changed hashes.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Adversarial critique: if implementation only compares abstract graph facts supplied by a parser, Integration might work; behavior explicitly targets rebase/squash hash-change realities, which are most reliably caught with real git behavior.
